title: Pre-Interview Checklist
description: Complete checklist for the day before and day of your interview. Covers technical prep, logistics, and mental preparation.

items.task: Review the job description and identify key requirements
Research the company: recent news, products, engineering blog, culture
Prepare 10-15 STAR stories covering different competencies
Practice 2-3 system design problems end-to-end
Review your resume and be ready to discuss every bullet point
Prepare thoughtful questions for each interviewer
Confirm interview schedule, format (virtual/onsite), and interviewer names
Test your coding environment (if virtual): IDE, screen sharing, audio/video
Review your STAR stories one final time — don't try to memorize, just refresh
Lay out clothes (business casual unless told otherwise)
Charge all devices: laptop, phone, backup charger
Set up your interview space: clean background, good lighting, quiet room
Test internet connection speed (minimum 10 Mbps up/down for video)
Prepare water, snacks, notepad, and pen
Review the company's core values and recent announcements
Get a good night's sleep — 7-8 hours minimum
Set 2 alarms for the morning
Eat a balanced breakfast with protein (avoid heavy foods)
Review your notes for 15-20 minutes — no more (avoid cramming)
Light exercise or stretching to reduce anxiety
Shower and dress professionally — it affects your mindset even on video
Close unnecessary browser tabs and applications
Silence phone and disable notifications on computer
Open the video call link 10 minutes early to verify everything works
Smile and make eye contact (look at the camera, not the screen)
Think out loud — explain your reasoning as you work
Clarify requirements before diving into solutions
Ask questions when stuck — interviewers want to help
Manage your time — check in with the interviewer on pacing
Take a breath before answering behavioral questions — structure your response
Write clean code with meaningful variable names
Test your solution with examples before saying you're done
Send a brief thank-you email to your recruiter within 24 hours
Write down the questions you were asked while they're fresh
Note what went well and what you'd improve for self-reflection
Don't obsess over perceived mistakes — interviewers weigh the full picture
Follow up with your recruiter if you haven't heard back within the stated timeline
